data.title: Ethena assures users of solvency after Bybit hack
data.description:
Ethena Labs has assured users that its yield-bearing stablecoin, Ethena USDe (USDe), remains solvent after an exploit drained more than a billion dollars from crypto exchange Bybit on Feb. 21.

According to the decentralized finance (DeFi) protocol, its synthetic dollar token, USDe, has roughly $30 million of exposure to financial derivatives on Bybit. However, the protocol claimed in an X post that its reserve fund is more than enough to offset any losses from the exploit. Bybit was hacked for over $1.4 billion in Lido Staked Ether (sTETH), Mantle Staked Ether (mETH) and other cryptocurrencies. 

Hedging against crypto market volatility using offchain derivatives plays a central role in Ethena Labs’ strategy for generating yield on USDe. 

“Currently there is <$30m of aggregate unrealised PNL relating to Bybit hedge positions, which is less than half of the reserve fund,” Ethena said, adding that: “USDe remains more than fully collateralised at this time.”

Ethena’s reserve fund “acts as an additional margin of safety behind USDe.” It held approximately $46 million as of the fourth quarter of 2024, according to Ethena’s documentation.

The DeFi developer further assured users that none of USDe’s cryptocurrency backing is held on Bybit itself. Instead, the digital assets are held with an off-exchange custodian, Copper. 

“As a reminder: all spot assets backing USDe are held in off exchange custody solutions, including Bybit via Copper Clearloop for this precise reason,” Ethena said. 

Centralization risks

Ethena lets users mint USDe against tokens such as Bitcoin , Ether , liquid staking tokens and other stablecoins. It then hedges against the portfolio’s inherent volatility using offchain financial derivatives.

Launched in February, USDe bootstrapped billions of dollars from stablecoin holders pursuing double-digit returns from Ethena’s yield strategy. 

“Ethena harnesses yield from staked assets (think Lido’s stETH), plus the funding and basis spread from perpetuals and futures markets, passing these gains on to sUSDe holders,” crypto researcher Messari said in a December research note.

However, Ethena’s CeDeFi — centralized DeFi — trading strategy poses risks, including failures by offchain exchanges, custodians and settlement providers, Messari noted.

Following the high-profile Bybit hack, the exchange’s CEO, Ben Zhou, reassured customers that withdrawals are still open but may take several hours to process due to high congestion.

data.title: Warren Buffett's growing cash pile and 3 other things investors want to hear about in Berkshire's investor letter
data.description:
By William Watts

Once again, it's all about the cash.

Berkshire Hathaway Inc. on Saturday morning will release its annual report. But the main event, as usual, will be Chairman and Chief Executive Warren Buffett's annual investor letter, in which he typically expounds on Berkshire's performance along with anecdotes and vignettes about investing and life.

Berkshire's cash pile stood at more than $300 billion, a record, as of the end of its third quarter. Cathy Seifert, an analyst at CFRA, expects Saturday's annual report to show it at around $320 billion.

"Perhaps one of the most highly analyzed aspects of the Berkshire model is its acquisition strategy. The focus on acquisitions (or lack thereof) has seemingly increased along with Berkshire's level of 'dry powder,'" Seifert said in a note.

Mark Hulbert: Warren Buffett's 2025 letter to Berkshire shareholders is coming soon. Watch this number.

This may sound familiar. After all, investors a year ago were similarly focused on a Berkshire cash hoard that has only continued to grow. Investors have grown only more nervous, wondering if the pile of cash reflects a tacit fear of a market fall.

Yet there doesn't appear to be a clear-cut signal for the broader market from Berkshire's cash holdings.

See: Warren Buffett's growing cash hoard makes stock-market bulls nervous. Should it?

For his part, Buffett has emphasized that while Berkshire remains on the hunt for ways to deploy cash, it's been hard to find attractive opportunities. At last year's Berkshire annual meeting in May, he reiterated that the company doesn't make deals for the sake of making deals and that he was more comfortable sitting on cash with interest rates at 5% - though he noted Berkshire had been willing to sit when rates were low as well.

"We only swing at pitches we like," he said.

Seifert noted that while Berkshire has published a list of acquisition criteria, the company has repeatedly claimed not to have a set acquisition strategy.

Berkshire's Class A shares (BRK.A) are up around 5.8% so far in 2025 and its Class B shares (BRK.B) are up 6%, versus a 2.5% rise for the S&P 500 SPX.

What else do investors want to hear about? Seifert offered some other potential highlights:

Equity portfolio

Berkshire's latest 13-F filing reflected some modest sales andpurchases in its $267 billion (as of Dec. 31) equity portfolio, with the firm being a net seller of about $6 billion worth of equities in the final quarter of 2024, Seifert noted. The most notable purchase was around $1 billion of Constellation Brands Inc. shares (STZ), while Berkshire reduced its holdings of bank stocks.

Investors will parse Berkshire's moves and any takeaways offered by the chairman "very carefully, particularly given Berkshire's growing cash pile and the fact that we believe Buffett is still responsible for the majority of investment decisions," she said.

Age and succession

Buffett will turn 95 in August.

"We think Berkshire has adequately addressed succession issues with Greg Abel's ascension to the role of vice chairman of noninsuranceoperations (and Buffett heir apparent)," Seifert said Ajit Jain, who heads up insurance operations, has a crucial role but is rumored to be stepping down in the next few years.

"Against that backdrop, we believe investors would be well served to be better acquainted with the broader managerial bench at Berkshire," she said.

Tone

The letter comes during a period of heightened uncertainty for investors as they assess the potential for trade wars, U.S. fiscal policy and geopolitical turmoil. The tone of the letter is usually "fairly benign," Seifert noted, with Buffett typically sticking to the year's results and often some sort of "folksy parable."

But there have been exceptions - including after the Sept. 11, 2001, terrorist attacks and the 2007-2009 financial crisis - when Buffett has commented on macroeconomic events.

"Given the degree to which macro events could impact Berkshire's business prospects, he might do so this year," Seifert said.

-William Watts

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Phillips 66 SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Phillips 66, a diversified energy manufacturing and logistics company, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report, detailing its financial and operational performance over the past year. The report provides insights into the company's strategic initiatives, business highlights, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Sales and other operating revenues: Decreased by 3%, primarily due to lower prices for refined petroleum products and crude oil, partially offset by an increase in prices for NGL.

Net income attributable to Phillips 66: $2,117 million. The decrease in 2024 was primarily due to a decline in realized refining margins driven by lower market crack spreads, partially offset by lower income tax expense.

Income before income taxes: $2,675 million. This includes a summary of income (loss) before income taxes by operating segment with a reconciliation to net income attributable to Phillips 66.

Income tax expense: $500 million. Income tax expense decreased by 78% in 2024, primarily due to lower income before income taxes.

Net cash provided by operating activities: $4,191 million. This represents a $2.8 billion decrease compared with 2023, primarily due to lower earnings driven by a decline in realized refining margins, partially offset by more favorable working capital impacts.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments:

  • The Midstream segment, which includes Transportation and NGL businesses, reported income before income taxes of $2,638 million in 2024, with Transportation contributing $1,292 million and NGL contributing $1,346 million.
  • The Chemicals segment, consisting of a 50% interest in CPChem, reported income before income taxes of $876 million in 2024, driven by improved margins and increased sales volumes.
  • The Refining segment experienced a loss before income taxes of $365 million in 2024, primarily due to lower realized margins as a result of declining market crack spreads.
  • The Marketing and Specialties segment reported income before income taxes of $1,011 million in 2024, with a decrease primarily driven by litigation-related expenses and lower U.S. marketing fuel margins.
  • The Renewable Fuels segment reported a loss before income taxes of $198 million in 2024, impacted by higher costs related to the ramp-up of the Rodeo Complex.

Geographical Performance:

  • The West Coast refining operations reported a significant loss before income taxes of $908 million in 2024, reflecting challenges in market conditions and operational adjustments.

Sales Units:

  • The Midstream segment reported pipeline throughput of 754 thousand barrels daily to major fractionation market hubs, with NGL production at 436 thousand barrels daily in 2024.
  • The Chemicals segment's CPChem reported externally marketed sales volumes of 24,088 million pounds in 2024, reflecting increased production and sales activities.
  • The Refining segment processed 1,743 thousand barrels daily of crude oil in 2024, with a worldwide capacity utilization rate of 95%.

New Production Launches:

  • The conversion of the San Francisco Refinery into the Rodeo Renewable Energy Complex was completed in 2024, marking a significant shift towards renewable fuels production.

Future Outlook:

  • The company plans to focus on operational excellence, targeting an annual clean product yield of greater than 86% and crude oil capacity utilization rates higher than the industry average for 2025-2027.
  • The Midstream segment aims to grow its NGL wellhead-to-market platform, with a budget of $975 million for 2025, including $546 million for growth projects.
  • The company is targeting a reduction in total debt to $17 billion and aims to maintain financial strength and flexibility while optimizing its asset portfolio.

Strategic Initiatives

Phillips 66 has focused on strategic priorities such as enhancing shareholder returns, improving refining performance, and expanding its Midstream and Chemicals businesses. The company completed the conversion of the San Francisco Refinery into the Rodeo Renewable Energy Complex and expanded its Midstream NGL wellhead-to-market platform with the acquisition of Pinnacle Midland Parent LLC. Additionally, Phillips 66 is targeting operational excellence by optimizing utilization rates and product yield at its refineries.

Capital Management:

  • In 2024, Phillips 66 generated $4.2 billion in cash from operations, funded capital expenditures and investments of $1.9 billion, and completed acquisitions for $625 million.
  • The company repurchased $3.5 billion of common stock and paid $1.9 billion in dividends.
  • Phillips 66 issued $3.3 billion in senior unsecured notes and extinguished $1.1 billion of senior notes using government obligations.
  • The company also entered into a $5 billion revolving credit agreement and maintained a $5 billion commercial paper program.

Future Outlook:

  • Phillips 66 plans to continue focusing on creating shareholder value and driving disciplined growth and returns. The company has budgeted $2.1 billion for 2025 capital expenditures and investments, with a focus on sustaining and growth projects in its Midstream and Refining segments.
  • The company aims to maintain financial strength and flexibility, targeting reductions in total debt to $17 billion and a debt-to-capital ratio.
  • Additionally, Phillips 66 is exploring opportunities to rationalize its asset portfolio and enhance its renewable fuels capabilities.

Challenges and Risks

Phillips 66 faces several significant risks that could impact its business operations and financial performance. Key risks include:

  • Market Risks: The cyclical and volatile nature of product margins due to changes in market conditions, which are largely beyond the company's control, directly affects earnings, financial condition, and cash flows. The prices for crude oil, natural gas, NGLs, and renewable feedstocks fluctuate based on global, regional, and local market conditions, impacting refining, wholesale marketing, and retail operations.
  • Operational Risks: The company's operations are subject to planned and unplanned downtime, business interruptions, and operational hazards, which could adversely impact financial condition, results of operations, and cash flows. Reliance on third-party transportation for crude oil and other products poses risks of supply interruptions and increased costs.
  • Regulatory Risks: Changes to government policies relating to renewable feedstocks and fuels, such as the renewable fuels standards program, could negatively impact financial condition and results of operations. Compliance with the EPA’s Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS) and other environmental regulations could result in increased operating costs and reduced demand for refined petroleum products.
  • Emerging Risks: Public health crises, such as pandemics, could materially and adversely impact the business. Additionally, societal, technological, political, and scientific developments around emissions and fuel efficiency may decrease demand for petroleum-based fuels.
  • Cybersecurity and Data Privacy Risks: Cybersecurity incidents and other disruptions could compromise information and expose the company to liability, causing business and reputational harm. Increasing regulatory focus on privacy and cybersecurity issues could expose the company to increased liability and restrictions on operations.
  • Indebtedness and Financial Risks: Uncertainty and illiquidity in credit and capital markets can impair the ability to obtain credit and financing on acceptable terms. Negative sentiment towards fossil fuels and increased attention to environmental and social matters could adversely affect business, market price for securities, and access to capital.

Management has identified several strategic priorities to address these challenges and risks:

  • Shareholder Returns: The company aims to enhance shareholder value through a secure, competitive, and growing dividend, complemented by share repurchases.
  • World-Class Operations: Focus on achieving operational excellence by optimizing utilization rates and product yield at refineries through reliable and safe operations.
  • Disciplined Growth and Returns: A disciplined capital allocation process ensures investment in projects expected to generate competitive returns, with a focus on growing Midstream and Chemicals businesses.
  • Financial Strength and Flexibility: The company uses a variety of funding sources to support liquidity requirements, including cash from operations, debt, and proceeds from dispositions.

The company is exposed to market risks related to commodity prices, interest rates, and foreign currency exchange rates. These risks are managed through a combination of derivative instruments and other financial strategies to mitigate potential adverse impacts on financial performance.

SEC Filing: Phillips 66 [ PSX ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: NV5 Global, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
NV5 Global, Inc., a leading provider of professional and technical engineering and consulting solutions, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces in the competitive engineering and consulting industry.

Financial Highlights

NV5 Global, Inc. reported significant financial metrics for the year 2024:

  • Gross revenues: $941.3 million, a 10% increase compared to 2023, driven by acquisitions and organic growth in international engineering and consulting services.
  • Gross profit: $483.2 million, representing a gross profit margin of 51.3%, up from 49.7% in 2023, due to a decrease in direct costs as a percentage of revenue.
  • Income from operations: $43.4 million, down from $60.0 million in 2023, primarily due to increased payroll, general and administrative, and amortization expenses.
  • Net income: $28.0 million, a 36% decrease compared to 2023, mainly due to higher operating expenses and interest expense.
  • Earnings per share (Basic): $0.45, reflecting the impact of decreased net income.
  • Earnings per share (Diluted): $0.44, also reflecting the impact of decreased net income.

Business Highlights

NV5 Global's business performance in 2024 was marked by several key operational metrics and initiatives:

  • Revenue Segments: The company's operations are organized into three segments: Infrastructure (INF), Building, Technology & Sciences (BTS), and Geospatial Solutions (GEO). INF includes engineering, civil program management, utility services, and conformity assessment practices. BTS covers clean energy consulting, data center commissioning, buildings and program management, MEP & technology design, and environmental health sciences. GEO focuses on geospatial solution practices.
  • Geographical Performance: NV5 Global operates over 100 locations in the U.S. and abroad, leveraging local market expertise. Each office is staffed with licensed professionals who understand the regional markets they serve, enhancing client engagement and service delivery.
  • Sales Units: The company serves approximately 11,300 clients, with its ten largest clients accounting for about 25% of gross revenues in 2024. Public and quasi-public sector clients represent a significant portion of the revenue base.
  • New Product Launches: NV5 Global has expanded its service offerings to include proprietary Lifecycle Commissioning® and subscription-based energy efficiency services, reflecting a commitment to innovation and sustainability.
  • Future Outlook: NV5 Global intends to continue focusing on public sector clients while building private sector capabilities. The company plans to strengthen its human capital, pursue strategic acquisitions, and expand its service offerings to private sector clients to enhance growth and market share.

Strategic Initiatives

NV5 Global, Inc. undertook several strategic initiatives in 2024 to bolster its market position and financial stability:

  • The company completed eleven acquisitions with a total purchase price of $86,852, including cash, promissory notes, common stock, and potential earn-outs. These acquisitions aimed to expand the company's service offerings and market presence, particularly in the geospatial solutions and infrastructure sectors.
  • NV5 Global announced a 4-for-1 stock split to increase the liquidity of its shares.
  • The company utilized its Senior Credit Facility to finance acquisitions and manage its capital structure. As of December 28, 2024, the outstanding balance on the facility was $232,750. NV5 Global also engaged in stock-based compensation and issued shares for acquisitions, reflecting its strategy to use equity as a tool for growth and employee retention.
  • NV5 Global plans to continue leveraging its Senior Credit Facility for strategic acquisitions and capital investments. The company anticipates that its sources of liquidity, including cash flows from operations and borrowing capacity, will be sufficient to meet its cash requirements for the next twelve months. The focus will remain on expanding service offerings and enhancing shareholder value through strategic acquisitions and effective capital management.

Challenges and Risks

NV5 Global faces several challenges and risks in its operations:

  • The company operates in a highly competitive industry with numerous small and large competitors, which may impact its ability to secure contracts and maintain market share.
  • Reliance on public sector clients exposes NV5 Global to risks related to government budget constraints and policy changes.
  • Operational risks include the loss of key personnel, cybersecurity threats, and the challenges of integrating acquisitions.
  • The reliance on a limited number of clients for a significant portion of revenue poses a risk if these clients reduce or terminate their contracts.
  • Regulatory risks include compliance with government procurement laws and potential liabilities under environmental laws.
  • The company's variable rate indebtedness exposes it to interest rate risk, and restrictive covenants in its credit agreements may limit operational flexibility.
  • Emerging risks include the impact of economic uncertainties and potential disruptions from catastrophic events.

Management has identified the need to strengthen human capital and pursue strategic acquisitions as key strategies to mitigate risks and drive growth. The company aims to balance its public and private sector client base to reduce dependency on government contracts. Efforts to enhance operational efficiency through a centralized shared services platform are also highlighted as a strategy to address competitive pressures.

SEC Filing: NV5 Global, Inc. [ NVEE ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: EXPEDITORS INTERNATIONAL OF WASHINGTON INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Expeditors International of Washington Inc., a global logistics company specializing in airfreight, ocean freight, and customs brokerage services, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive logistics industry.

Financial Highlights

Expeditors International of Washington Inc. reported strong financial results for 2024, driven by increased demand in its core services:

  • Revenues: $10,600,515 million, reflecting a 13% increase in airfreight services, a 33% increase in ocean freight and ocean services, and a 2% increase in customs brokerage and other services compared to 2023.
  • Operating Income: $1,041,323 million, an 11% increase from 2023, driven by higher revenues and effective cost management.
  • Net Earnings: $811,633 million, an 8% increase from 2023, supported by strong demand in airfreight and ocean freight services.
  • Net Earnings Attributable to Shareholders: $810,073 million, reflecting an 8% increase from 2023, with significant contributions from international operations.

Business Highlights

Expeditors' business performance in 2024 was marked by significant growth across its primary service segments and geographical regions:

  • Revenue Segments: Airfreight services accounted for approximately 34% of total revenues, Ocean Freight and Ocean Services contributed 30%, and Customs Brokerage and Other Services made up 36%.
  • Geographical Performance: North Asia was the largest export-oriented region, accounting for 28% of revenues. South Asia and North Asia saw significant increases in airfreight tonnage due to high demand in the technology sector and manufacturing relocations. Europe and MAIR regions experienced growth in customs brokerage and other services due to higher shipment volumes.
  • Sales Units: Ocean containers shipped increased by 7%, and airfreight tonnage was up 12% compared to 2023, driven by demand in the technology sector and e-commerce.
  • Future Outlook: Expeditors anticipates continued pricing volatility in ocean freight as carriers adapt to changes in demand, fuel prices, and new regulations. The company expects rate declines to continue into 2025 as demand softens and capacity increases. Additionally, Expeditors plans to increase spending on cybersecurity, IT infrastructure, and strategic growth initiatives.

Strategic Initiatives

Expeditors has focused on several strategic initiatives to enhance its operational efficiency and market position:

  • Technology and Infrastructure Investments: The company has invested in technology and infrastructure improvements, including cybersecurity, internal controls, and IT infrastructure upgrades, to support growth and improve service delivery across its global logistics network.
  • Capital Management: Expeditors actively managed its capital through a Discretionary Stock Repurchase Plan, repurchasing 7.1 million shares in 2024 at an average price of $119.47 per share. The company returned $1,059 million to shareholders through stock repurchases and dividends, declaring semi-annual dividends of $0.73 per share, up from $0.69 per share in 2023. Expeditors maintains a strong liquidity position with $1,148 million in cash and cash equivalents and no long-term debt, aside from lease liabilities.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Expeditors plans to continue its strategic investments in technology and infrastructure to support growth and enhance operational efficiency. The company anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $50 million in 2025, focusing on routine capital needs and technology investments. Expeditors also expects to manage its capital structure prudently, maintaining a strong cash position to support future growth opportunities and shareholder returns.

Challenges and Risks

Expeditors faces several challenges and risks in the highly competitive global logistics industry:

  • Industry Risks: The company operates in a competitive environment influenced by economic and political conditions, currency exchange rates, and trade policies. It faces competition from new technology-based competitors with substantial capital funding, niche players, and larger entities. Industry consolidations are also a factor, with Expeditors emphasizing organic growth supplemented by select strategic acquisitions.
  • Operational Risks: Expeditors is exposed to operational risks such as dependency on personnel, technological disruptions, and reliance on service providers. The company has identified material weaknesses in internal control over financial reporting, which could impact investor confidence and stock price.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with complex international regulations and potential changes in tax laws pose significant risks. Additionally, climate change and related regulations present long-term risks to operations and financial results.
  • Market and Economic Risks: The global economic and trade environments remain uncertain, with inflation, oil price volatility, and geopolitical conflicts affecting operations. Expeditors experienced capacity constraints and pricing volatility in 2024, particularly in ocean and airfreight services. The company is adapting to changes in demand and trade policies, but future impacts from evolving e-commerce platforms and shifts in manufacturing locations due to tariffs and geopolitical issues remain uncertain.

SEC Filing: EXPEDITORS INTERNATIONAL OF WASHINGTON INC [ EXPD ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: American Homes 4 Rent SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
American Homes 4 Rent (AMH), a Maryland-based Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) specializing in single-family rental homes, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Rents and other single-family property revenues: $1,728.7 million, driven primarily by higher rental rates.

Net income: $468.1 million, attributed to growth in rents and other single-family property revenues, higher net gains on property sales, and an increase in other income and expense, net.

Core NOI: $978.3 million, providing useful information about the operating performance of single-family properties without the impact of certain reimbursed operating expenses.

Same-Home Core NOI: $870.4 million, primarily due to a 5.3% increase in Average Monthly Realized Rent per property, reaching $2,189 per month.

FFO attributable to common share and unit holders: $690.7 million, an important measure for real estate companies as it excludes depreciation.

Core FFO attributable to common share and unit holders: $743.6 million, allowing investors to compare operating performance across periods without the effect of non-comparable items.

Adjusted FFO attributable to common share and unit holders: $663.3 million, adjusted for Recurring Capital Expenditures and leasing costs.

EBITDA: $1,110.5 million, used as a supplemental measure of performance.

EBITDAre: $889.5 million, adjusted for gains and losses from sales or impairments of single-family properties.

Adjusted EBITDAre: $942.3 million, adjusted for acquisition and other transaction costs, noncash share-based compensation expense, hurricane-related charges, and loss on early extinguishment of debt.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: American Homes 4 Rent (AMH) focuses on acquiring, developing, renovating, leasing, and managing single-family homes as rental properties. The company operates primarily through its Operating Partnership.

Geographical Performance: As of December 31, 2024, AMH owned 61,336 single-family properties across 24 states, with significant holdings in markets such as Atlanta, Charlotte, Dallas-Fort Worth, Phoenix, and Nashville. The company also held 3,376 properties in unconsolidated joint ventures.

Sales Units: During 2024, AMH developed or acquired 3,724 homes, including 2,000 newly constructed homes through its AMH Development Program and 1,673 homes through a bulk portfolio acquisition.

Property Management: AMH manages its properties internally, leveraging a comprehensive property management platform that includes local leasing agents and centralized corporate functions to optimize rental revenues and manage expenses.

New Production Launches: The AMH Development Program focuses on constructing 'built-for-rental' homes, designed with long-term renters in mind. In 2024, the company delivered 2,356 homes through this program, including contributions to joint ventures.

Operational Strategy: AMH employs a disciplined property acquisition process, focusing on homes built after 2000 with specific characteristics to ensure high occupancy and rental rates. The company also maintains a geographically diversified portfolio to mitigate market risks.

Future Outlook: AMH plans to continue its growth through internal development and strategic acquisitions, focusing on high-growth markets and leveraging its property management platform to enhance operational efficiency.

Seasonality: The company experiences higher tenant turnover during late spring and summer, impacting rental revenues and turnover costs. Property operating costs are also seasonally affected by weather conditions in certain markets.

Human Capital Management: AMH emphasizes attracting and retaining a skilled workforce, offering comprehensive benefits and training programs. The company reported a 25.1% employee turnover rate in 2024 and provided 70,200 hours of training.

Regulatory Compliance: AMH ensures compliance with various housing regulations, including the Fair Housing Act, and actively monitors legislative developments related to residential housing laws.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: The company has strategically scaled back acquisitions of single-family properties through its National Builder Program and traditional acquisition channels as the housing market adjusts to the current macroeconomic environment. It continues to focus on developing 'built-for-rental' homes through its internal AMH Development Program and acquiring newly constructed homes from third-party developers. The company also plans to repay all amounts due under the AMH 2015-SFR1 and AMH 2015-SFR2 securitizations in 2025.

Capital Management: The company issued $1.59 billion in unsecured senior notes during 2024 and entered into a new $1.25 billion sustainability-linked revolving credit facility. It also raised $142.7 million through financing activities, including equity issuances under its 2023 At-the-Market Program, which raised $33.7 million in gross proceeds. The company did not repurchase any shares under its share repurchase program, which has a remaining authorization of $265.1 million for Class A common shares and $250.0 million for preferred shares. The company distributed $450.8 million to shareholders in 2024, up from $378.5 million in 2023.

Future Outlook: The company intends to continue evaluating growth channels and will grow accordingly if acquisition opportunities are attractive relative to the condition of capital markets. It plans to use proceeds from its At-the-Market Program to repay debt, develop new properties, and for general corporate purposes. The company also aims to maintain its investment-grade credit rating to ensure greater availability and lower cost of debt financing.

Challenges and Risks

Market Risks: The company is exposed to fluctuations in rental demand and property values, particularly in its concentrated geographic markets. A downturn in these markets could significantly impact AMH's financial performance.

Operational Risks: High inflation and rising property taxes are increasing operational costs. The company may not be able to offset these costs through higher rents, which could adversely affect its operating results.

Regulatory Risks: New laws and regulations, including those related to tenant rights and rent control, could impede AMH's ability to operate or grow its business. Additionally, HOA rules and restrictions may increase costs and limit business operations.

Emerging Risks: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) introduces potential operational challenges and reputational risks. AMH may also face increasing regulations related to AI usage.

Environmental Risks: Climate change poses direct risks such as property damage from extreme weather events and indirect risks from regulatory changes aimed at reducing carbon emissions.

Cybersecurity Risks: AMH is highly dependent on information systems, and any failures or breaches could disrupt operations and damage its reputation.

Financial Risks: Elevated interest rates could increase borrowing costs and impact the company's growth prospects. Additionally, the company's revolving credit facility and unsecured senior notes contain covenants that could restrict business activities.

Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by high inflation and interest rates, which have increased operational costs and borrowing expenses. The company is focused on optimizing its capital structure and leveraging its internal development program to mitigate these risks.

Market Risk Management: AMH is exposed to interest rate risks, which could affect its financial condition and results of operations. The company is actively managing these risks through its capital structure and financial strategies.

SEC Filing: American Homes 4 Rent [ AMH ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Market Chatter: Equinor Exploring Sale of Onshore Assets in Argentina
data.description:
Equinor is considering divesting its onshore oil and gas assets in Argentina's Vaca Muerta region, Reuters reported Friday, citing Argentina-based publication publication Mas Energia.

The company has engaged Bank of America to facilitate the sale of its stakes in the Bandurria Sur and Bajo del Toro Norte licenses, in which it holds 30% and 50%, respectively, according to the report.

Equinor does not plan to sell its interests in offshore licenses in Argentina.

Bandurria Sur is currently producing around 70,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day, while Bajo del Toro Norte has yet to be developed, the report added.

Equinor did not immediately reply to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Boise Cascade Down Nearly 11%, on Pace for Largest Percent Decrease Since February 2022 — Data Talk

data.title: Reddit Stock Extends Its Slide. Why an Analyst Still Says It's a Buy. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Angela Palumbo

Reddit stock continued its slide Friday, but that didn't stop Piper Sandler from staying optimistic about the online social platform.

Piper Sandler analyst Thomas Champion increased his price target on Reddit to $220 from $210 on Friday while maintaining an Overweight rating.

The boosted price target comes despite a number of factors weighing on the stock lately.

Reddit reported on Feb. 12 that fourth-quarter daily active unique visitors rose less than Wall Street expected. Late in the quarter, the company experienced "some volatility from Google search triggered by a periodic algorithm change," Chief Executive Officer Steven Huffman said on the earnings call.

While data did show a decline in feed audience in December, "this bounced back in January," Champion wrote.

He isn't alone in his bullishness. Of the 24 analysts surveyed by FactSet, 15 say the stock is a Buy, eight say it's a Hold, and one rates it Sell. Their average price target on the stock is $209.57.

Investors continue to remain skeptical, though. The stock slid 6.8% on Friday to $163.56. Shares are now down 27% from their all-time closing high of $225.23 on Feb. 7.

Reddit stock has also faced increased pressure after breaking through its 50-day moving average on Thursday. It continued to trade roughly $20 below that key level on Friday.

When stocks trade below that average, it's one sign to investors that a continued downward trend is potentially in the cards.

News on Thursday that the Federal Trade Commission had launched a public inquiry into censorship by technology platforms didn't help either. Reddit and other social media stocks took a hit: Meta Platforms dropped 1.3% and Snapchat parent Snap declined 0.9% on Thursday.

Write to Angela Palumbo at angela.palumbo@dowjones.com

data.title: Dear Rigetti Computing Stock Fans, a MAJOR Military Catalyst Is Just Around the Corner
data.description:
Quantum computing is the new gold rush, and investors are eagerly watching the horizon for the next big breakthrough. With promises of unmatched processing power, it is no wonder that the technology is causing such a stir. Rigetti Computing has made significant strides, reaching an impressive 99.5% fidelity by the end of last year. However, the real magic will begin once it scales those qubits. The company expects to achieve a narrow quantum advantage within three years and anticipates commercial adoption within five.

What’s even more exciting is, according to analysts, a new catalyst is on the horizon for Rigetti investors. The upcoming Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA) Quantum Benchmarking contract awards anticipated in February could be a game-changer, and analysts believe Rigetti is perfectly positioned to secure a win. With a projected value of $300 million over seven years, the DARPA initiative could pave the way for the development of high-fidelity, error-corrected qubit systems.

About Rigetti Stock

Rigetti Computing RGTI), based in Berkeley, California, is making waves as a leader in full-stack quantum-classical computing. With a market cap of around $2.2 billion, the company’s proprietary quantum-classical infrastructure seamlessly integrates with both public and private clouds, pushing the boundaries of what quantum computing can achieve.

The company’s growth in 2024 was nothing short of extraordinary amid all the exciting developments in the quantum computing space. Over the past 52 weeks, RGTI’s shares have surged by a stunning 595%, dwarfing the broader S&P 500 Index's ($SPX) 23% gain. In fact, over the past six months alone, the stock has rocketed an astonishing 1,040%.

While Rigetti has undoubtedly delivered a dazzling performance over the past year, the momentum comes with a hefty price tag. Presently trading at an eye-watering 280.68 times forward sales, which is significantly above the sector median of 3.27x, this sky-high valuation reflects the market’s immense belief in Rigetti’s future.

A Closer Look at Rigetti’s Q3 Performance

In November 2024, Rigetti dropped its third-quarter 2024 financial results, marking a crucial point in the company’s journey. For the three months that ended on Sept. 30, 2024, Rigetti’s total revenues were $2.4 million, reflecting a 23% year-over-year dip and falling well below Wall Street’s forecast figure of $3.4 million.

The company’s operating expenses for the period totaled $18.6 million, resulting in a net loss of $14.8 million. On the brighter side, although the company incurred a loss of $0.08 per share, the reported figure marked a significant improvement from the loss of $0.17 per share in the year-ago quarter. Plus, despite incurring losses, Rigetti remains in a strong financial position, with cash, cash equivalents, and securities amounting to $92.6 million as of Sept. 30.

Looking ahead, Rigetti is set to make significant strides on the technical front. In 2025, the company plans to introduce a new modular system architecture, with a 36-qubit system expected by mid-year and a system of over 100 qubits by year-end. Both systems will target a 99.5% median 2-qubit fidelity, marking a major milestone in quantum computing. Additionally, Rigetti aims to develop the 336-qubit Lyra system in the years that follow.

Analysts tracking Rigetti project the company’s loss to narrow a notable 35.9% year over year in fiscal 2024 and improve another 17.8% in fiscal 2025.

What Do Analysts Expect for Rigetti Stock?

RGTI is about to light up the stock market. Alliance Global analyst Brian Kinstlinger has some thrilling predictions. With the highly anticipated DARPA Quantum Benchmarking contract awards decision arriving in February, Kinstlinger is convinced Rigetti is "positioned to be one of the winners,” crediting the company’s strides in fidelity rates, tiling innovations, and recent wins from other DoD research labs.

Kinstlinger sees this potential DARPA deal as more than just a win. It is a massive validation for RGTI. He believes it could lead to a profitable future. With that in mind, the analyst assigned a “Buy” rating and raised his price target for Rigetti shares from $5.50 to $15. This newly raised forecast suggests an exciting 30% upside in the months ahead.

While Wall Street coverage might be limited, the consensus stands at a “Strong Buy.” Among five analysts covering RGTI, four advocate a “Strong Buy,” while one recommends a “Moderate Buy.” RGTI’s average analyst price target of $12.30 represents potential upside of 7.7%, while the Street-high target of $17 suggests that the stock can climb as much as 48.9% from here.

data.title: Ryman Hospitality Properties, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Ryman Hospitality Properties, Inc., a self-advised and self-administered Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) specializing in group-oriented, destination hotel assets in urban and resort markets, has released its annual 10-K report. The report details the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive hospitality industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $2,339.2 million, reflecting a strong increase from the previous year driven by growth in rooms, food and beverage, and entertainment segments.
  • Operating Income: $490.8 million, indicating improved operational efficiency and cost management.
  • Net Income: $280.2 million, showcasing a robust financial performance despite increased interest expenses.
  • Basic Income Per Share Available to Common Stockholders: $4.54, demonstrating significant earnings growth per share.
  • Diluted Income Per Share Available to Common Stockholders: $4.38, reflecting the company's ability to generate earnings on a per-share basis.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Ryman Hospitality Properties, Inc. operates as a self-advised and self-administered REIT specializing in group-oriented, destination hotel assets in urban and resort markets. The company transitioned to a REIT structure on January 1, 2013, to optimize tax efficiency.
  • Hospitality Segment: The company owns and operates a network of five upscale, meetings-focused resorts under the Gaylord Hotels brand, managed by Marriott. These properties are located in key urban and resort markets across the United States, including Nashville, Orlando, Dallas, Washington D.C., and Denver.
  • Entertainment Segment: Ryman holds a controlling 70% equity interest in the Opry Entertainment Group, which includes iconic assets such as the Grand Ole Opry, Ryman Auditorium, and various themed entertainment venues. This segment contributes significantly to the company's diversified revenue streams.
  • Hotel Portfolio Expansion: In June 2023, Ryman acquired the JW Marriott San Antonio Hill Country Resort & Spa, expanding its hospitality footprint and enhancing its portfolio of group-oriented hotels.
  • Property Enhancements: The company is actively investing in its existing properties, with a $98 million enhancement project completed at Gaylord Rockies in 2024 and a $225 million multi-phase improvement plan underway at Gaylord Opryland, including significant expansions in meeting space.
  • Brand Leverage: Ryman capitalizes on the strong brand recognition of the Grand Ole Opry to drive revenue through strategic partnerships and brand extensions, including new entertainment venues and media collaborations.
  • Future Outlook: Ryman aims to be the premier hospitality REIT for group-oriented, destination hotel assets, focusing on strategic acquisitions, property enhancements, and leveraging its entertainment brand to drive growth and shareholder value.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company undertook significant strategic initiatives, including the acquisition of JW Marriott Hill Country for approximately $800 million, funded through a combination of a public offering, senior notes issuance, and cash on hand. Additionally, the company completed the purchase of Block 21, a mixed-use entertainment complex, for $255 million, which included the assumption of existing mortgage debt. These acquisitions are part of the company's strategy to expand its portfolio of upscale, meetings-focused resorts and entertainment assets.
  • Capital Management: The company engaged in several capital management activities, including the issuance of $400 million in senior notes and a public offering of approximately 4.4 million shares to fund the JW Marriott Hill Country acquisition. It also managed its debt portfolio by repaying $800 million under the Gaylord Rockies term loan and issuing $1 billion in senior notes. The company declared dividends totaling $4.45 per share/unit for the year and maintained a dividend policy to distribute 100% of REIT taxable income annually. No shares were repurchased during the year.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, the company plans to maintain its REIT status by distributing at least 90% of its taxable income and aims to continue its strategic focus on expanding its hospitality and entertainment segments. The company is also exploring opportunities for further acquisitions and investments in its existing properties to enhance shareholder value and support sustainable growth.

Challenges and Risks

  • Operational Risks: The company's reliance on Marriott as a third-party hotel manager concentrates operational risk. Any failure by Marriott to manage the properties effectively could negatively impact financial results. Additionally, the geographic concentration of hotel properties increases vulnerability to local economic conditions and natural disasters.
  • Market Risks: The hospitality industry is highly competitive, and Ryman's focus on the group-oriented meetings sector makes it susceptible to downturns in this niche market. The opening of new hotels, including those owned by competitors, could impact Ryman's market share.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with REIT requirements and other regulations, such as the Americans with Disabilities Act and environmental laws, could impose significant costs. Changes in tax laws or failure to maintain REIT status could adversely affect financial performance.
  • Financial Risks: Ryman's substantial debt load could limit financial flexibility and increase vulnerability to interest rate fluctuations. The need to refinance debt under unfavorable conditions could impact liquidity and operational capabilities.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: The reliance on information technology systems exposes Ryman to risks of data breaches and cyberattacks, which could lead to operational disruptions and reputational damage.
  • Emerging Risks: Inflationary pressures and labor shortages could increase operating costs, while changes in privacy and data security laws could raise compliance costs and litigation risks.

SEC Filing: Ryman Hospitality Properties, Inc. [ RHP ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: CoreCivic, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
CoreCivic, Inc., a leading provider of diversified government solutions in the United States, has released its annual 10-K report for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2024. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces in the correctional services industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $1,961.6 million, reflecting a 3.4% increase from the previous year, driven by higher average revenue per compensated man-day and increased occupancy.
  • Net Income: $68.9 million, compared with $67.6 million in the previous year, indicating a slight improvement in profitability.
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.62 per diluted share, up from $0.59 per diluted share in the previous year, reflecting improved earnings per share.
  • Operating Income: Not explicitly stated, but operating income per compensated man-day increased to $24.34 from $21.47, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Gross Profit: Not explicitly stated, but operating margin increased to 24.0% from 21.9%, suggesting enhanced profitability.

Business Highlights

  • Business Segments: CoreCivic operates through three main segments: CoreCivic Safety, CoreCivic Community, and CoreCivic Properties. CoreCivic Safety is the largest segment, operating 42 correctional and detention facilities with a design capacity of 62,329 beds. CoreCivic Community operates 21 residential reentry centers with a design capacity of 4,159 beds. CoreCivic Properties owns 6 correctional properties with a design capacity of 10,314 beds.
  • Revenue Segments: For the year ended December 31, 2024, the CoreCivic Safety segment generated 91.1% of the total segment net operating income, CoreCivic Community generated 4.6%, and CoreCivic Properties generated 4.3%.
  • Customer Contracts: CoreCivic's customer contracts typically have terms of one to five years with multiple renewal options. The contract renewal rate for properties owned or controlled via long-term lease was approximately 96% over the five years ended December 31, 2024.
  • Occupancy Rates: The average compensated occupancy of CoreCivic Safety and Community facilities was 75% in 2024, with occupancy rates excluding idled facilities at 86%.
  • Reentry Programs: CoreCivic has committed to evolving its model with an increased focus on reentry services, offering a wide range of evidence-based reentry programs and activities, including educational and vocational training.
  • New Programs and Initiatives: In 2024, CoreCivic developed the Go Further Discovery learning management system and implemented several new programs, such as the Escape the Odds Box Truck Program and Promising People Virtual Reality programs.
  • Federal and State Contracts: Federal customers, including ICE and USMS, accounted for 51% of total revenue in 2024. State revenues constituted 40% of total revenue, with Tennessee being the largest state customer.
  • Facility Management Contracts: CoreCivic signed new management contracts with Hinds County, Mississippi, the state of Wyoming, and Harris County, Texas, among others, demonstrating the flexible solutions provided to government partners.
  • Future Outlook: CoreCivic expects demand from the federal government, particularly from ICE, to increase due to anticipated changes in immigration policy and funding levels. The company is also exploring opportunities to expand non-residential correctional alternatives.
  • Idle Facilities: CoreCivic has 13,419 beds at nine correctional and detention facilities that are vacant and available for growth opportunities.
  • Community Corrections: CoreCivic is the second largest community corrections owner and operator in the U.S., with a focus on employment, job readiness, and life skills to help offenders successfully re-enter the community.
  • Real Estate Solutions: CoreCivic offers flexible real estate solutions, including leasing correctional facilities to government and third-party operators, and providing turn-key services for public correctional systems in need of replacement capacity.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: CoreCivic has focused on strategic initiatives to modernize correctional infrastructure and expand its service offerings. This includes entering into new management contracts with various states and counties, such as Montana, Wyoming, and Harris County, Texas, to provide correctional services. The company is also exploring real estate solutions for government agencies with aged infrastructure, as demonstrated by the lease of the Allen Gamble Correctional Center to the Oklahoma Department of Corrections.
  • Capital Management: CoreCivic has been actively managing its capital through debt refinancing and share repurchases. The company completed a tender offer and redemption of its 8.25% senior unsecured notes, replacing them with new notes due 2029. It also increased its revolving credit facility and term loan capacity. Additionally, CoreCivic has repurchased 14.5 million shares of its common stock at a total cost of $181.1 million under its share repurchase program, which was expanded to $350 million.
  • Future Outlook: CoreCivic plans to continue its focus on debt reduction and returning capital to shareholders through share repurchases. The company is also poised to capitalize on potential growth opportunities in providing real estate and management solutions to government agencies. With no debt maturities until 2027, CoreCivic is well-positioned to pursue strategic investments and respond to emerging needs in the correctional services market.

Challenges and Risks

  • Regulatory and Political Risks: The company faces significant risks related to the privatization of correctional, detention, and residential reentry facilities. Resistance from labor unions, prison reform organizations, and activists could impact the ability to obtain new contracts or renew existing ones. The Private Prison EO issued by President Biden, which directed the DOJ not to renew contracts with privately operated facilities, has already affected operations, although it was reversed by President Trump in 2025. Future administrations could reissue similar orders, impacting federal contracts.
  • Legislative Risks: Legislation in states like California, Colorado, and New Jersey restricts contracts for detaining immigrants, affecting operations in those regions. Negative publicity from disturbances or operational issues at facilities could harm the company's reputation and contract renewals.
  • Occupancy and Revenue Risks: The company is also subject to fluctuations in occupancy levels, which could decrease revenues and profitability, as a substantial portion of revenue is based on per diem payments. Government budgetary challenges and shutdowns could delay payments or lead to contract terminations, affecting cash flow and financial condition.
  • Financial Risks: Efforts to reduce the U.S. federal deficit and uncertainty regarding federal budget and spending levels could adversely affect liquidity and financial condition. Competition from government entities and other private operators could impact profitability, as could the non-renewal or competitive re-bidding of government contracts.
  • Customer Concentration Risks: The company is dependent on a limited number of governmental customers, with ICE, USMS, and BOP accounting for a significant portion of revenues. Any loss or reduction in these contracts could materially impact financial results.
  • Labor and Litigation Risks: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by labor shortages and wage pressures, which have led to increased staffing costs. The company has invested in workforce resources to manage increased resident numbers post-COVID-19 restrictions, but future labor market conditions remain uncertain. The company is also exposed to various types of litigation, including employment-related claims and those associated with facility management. Legal proceedings could result in significant costs and impact financial condition.
  • Market Risks: The company is subject to interest rate risks, as it has incurred variable rate debt. Rising interest rates could increase interest costs, affecting the ability to pay down debt and impacting financial results. Additionally, activist resistance to private correctional facilities could impact access to financing, potentially increasing the cost of capital and affecting business operations.

SEC Filing: CoreCivic, Inc. [ CXW ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: WTI Crude Oil Falls to a Near Two-Month Low as Winter Cold Abates and U.S. Inventories Swell
data.description:
West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude oil closed at the lowest since Dec.26 on Friday, dropping on weaker demand and robust supply as bitter winter cold in the northern United States fades and inventories rise.

WTI crude oil for April delivery closed down US$2.08 to settle at US$70.40 per barrel, while April Brent crude was last seen down US$1.84 to US$74.64.

Oil traders are adjusting to the start of a bearish outlook for the commodity, as U.S. winter demand eases following the end of a polar vortex that brought bitterly cold weather to northern states, raising demand and cutting supply as infrastructure froze. Demand growth from China, the No.1 importer remains weak and OPEC+ is sticking with a plan to gradually return 2.2-million barrels per day of production cuts to market starting in April, while Western hemisphere supply is growing.

The Energy Information Administration on Thursday reported commercial crude inventories rose by a more than expected 4.6-million barrels last week, though inventories of refined products dropped due to refinery maintenance and winter demand.

"With an elongated winter, a Distillate draw and an increase in product demand the refinery turnaround period is starting to leave a hole in product supply with utilization only running at 85%," PVM Oil Associates noted.

The prospect of easing sanctions on Russian exports as U.S. President Donald Trump warms to Vladimir Putin, his Russian counterpart, while turning against further support for Ukraine is also unsettling markets. Trump is also looking to boost U.S. production, which is already at a record 13.5-million barrels per day, while demand may fall if he follows through on plans to impose widespread tariffs on U.S. imports.

"The market also has to deal with an increasingly erratic message flow from Washington, which on one hand has raised the prospect of increased US production, while on the other causing concerns about the outlook for global growth and demand," Ole Hansen, head of commodity strategy at Saxo Bank, noted.

data.title: MeiraGTx Shares Hit 52-Week High After Blindness-Treatment Data Release
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

Shares of MeiraGTx Holdings shares reached a 52-week high after the company said 11 children treated to date in a drug study gained visual acuity after treatment.

The stock was recently up 9.1%, to $7.45, Friday after touching an intraday high of $7.80. Shares are up about 23% since the start of the year.

The genetic-medicines company reported results from a study of rAAV8.hRKp.AIPL1 in children who were legally blind at birth with AIPL1-associated severe retinal dystrophy.

A paper published in The Lancet presents data for the first four, who were treated unilaterally. Following the strong safety and substantial efficacy demonstrated in this cohort, another seven have now been treated bilaterally.

"With these truly exceptional results in hand, we are working with global regulators on expedited approval of rAAV8.hRKp.AIPL1 to provide access to babies born with AIPL1-related LCA4," the company said.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com

data.title: Sugar Prices Extend a Month-Long Rally on Supply Uncertainty
data.description:
March NY world sugar #11 (SBH25) Friday closed up +0.26 (+1.24%), and May London ICE white sugar #5 (SWK25) closed up +4.80 (+0.86%).

Sugar prices Friday extended their month-long rally to 2-1/4 month highs. Sugar prices saw continued support from Monday's news that India's sugar production fell -12% yr/yr to 19.7 MMT in the marketing year-to-date from October 1 through February 15, according to the India Sugar and Bio-Energy Manufacturers Association.

The 2-month-long rally in the Brazilian real (^USDBRL) has discouraged export selling from Brazil's sugar producers and fueled fund short-covering in sugar futures. On Tuesday, the Brazilian real posted a 3-month high against the US dollar.

Sugar prices have a positive carryover from last Thursday when Alvean, the world's largest sugar trader, said that below-average precipitation in Brazil means sugarcane is underdeveloped in some areas, and if showers remain weak, the sugar harvest that begins in April could be delayed, and sugar production would suffer.

In a bearish factor, the Indian government said on January 20 that it would allow its sugar mills to export 1 MMT of sugar this season, easing the restrictions placed on sugar exports in 2023. India has restricted sugar exports since October 2023 to maintain adequate domestic supplies. India allowed mills to export only 6.1 MMT of sugar during the 2022/23 season to September 30 after allowing exports of a record 11.1 MMT in the previous season. However, the India Sugar Mills Association (ISMA) projects that India's 2024/25 sugar production will fall -15% y/y to a 5-year low of 27.27 MMT.

The outlook for higher sugar production in Thailand is bearish for sugar prices. On October 29, Thailand's Office of the Cane and Sugar Board projected that Thailand's 2024/25 sugar production would jump by +18% y/y to 10.35 MMT. Thailand produced 8.77 MMT of sugar in the 2023/24 season that ended in April. Thailand is the world's third-largest sugar producer and the second-largest sugar exporter.

Drought and excessive heat last year caused fires in Brazil that damaged sugar crops in Brazil's top sugar-producing state of Sao Paulo. Sugar cane industry group Orplana said that as many as 2,000 fire outbreaks affected up to 80,000 hectares of planted sugarcane in Sao Paulo. Green Pool Commodity Specialists noted that as much as 5 MMT of sugar cane may have been lost due to the fires. Conab, Brazil's government crop forecasting agency, cut its 2024/25 Brazil sugar production estimate from November 21 to 44 MMT from a previous forecast of 46 MMT, citing lower sugarcane yields due to drought and excessive heat. Unica reported Thursday that cumulative 2024/25 Center-South sugar output through January fell -5.5% y/y to 39.805 MMT.

The USDA, in its bi-annual report released November 21, projected that global 2024/25 sugar production would climb +1.5% y/y to a record 186.619 MMT and that global 2024/25 human sugar consumption would increase +1.2% y/y to a record 179.63 MMT. The USDA also forecasted that 2024/25 global sugar ending stocks would decline -6.1% y/y to 45.427 MMT.

Meanwhile, the International Sugar Organization (ISO) on November 21 forecasted a 2024/25 global sugar deficit of -2.51 MMT, showing a tightening market from the 2023/24 global sugar surplus of 1.31 MMT. For its part, Green Pool Commodity Specialists on February 5 projected that the global sugar market will shift to a surplus of +2.7 MMT in the 2025/26 crop year from its estimate of a deficit of -3.7 MMT in 2024/25.

data.title: Recent crypto liquidation scared off YouTube’s retail audience: Ran Neuner
data.description:
The latest wave of crypto liquidations has driven retail investors away from digital assets, according to Crypto Banter founder Ran Neuner, who described the market’s current state as “max pain.”

In an interview at Consensus Hong Kong 2025, Ran Neuner told Cointelegraph that when Bitcoin (BTC) reached 60% market dominance, investors quickly pivoted to altcoins, assuming the market would follow the traditional four-year cycle — only to be met with a massive liquidity flush.

The flush was triggered in part by the threat of trade wars between the US, Canada and Mexico, which led to a large market sell-off and at least $2.4 billion liquidated in 24 hours.

Altcoins, including Ether (ETH), suffered steep declines in prices. Neuner said no one thought the second-biggest cryptocurrency would crash so hard in one night, and “that put a lot of people off-guard.”

The situation may have scared off YouTube’s crypto retail audience. Neuner said good-quality Crypto Banter videos are getting between a quarter to one-third fewer views than they were during the 2021 crypto bull market. But he added that “where there’s money to be made, retail will arrive.”

While he is bullish on memecoins, he added that they may have been a hindrance to onboarding retail, as new investors “walked into the most corrupt casino in the world, just filled with insiders, snipe bots and whatever else. So, I think it’s going to be a long time before retail gets here, but they will come.”

Memecoins have been a particular lightning rod of controversy in the past week due to the LIBRA rug pull scandal. According to Nansen, investors saw a combined loss of around $251 million. Argentine President Javier Milei has been drawn into the controversy due to his role in posting about the LIBRA token on X.

As Cointelegraph reported on Feb. 21, Solana token launches are slowing significantly, with Pump.fun, a Solana memecoin launcher, posting its weakest revenue since early November 2024.

Neuner is expecting a much tamer cycle and much more protracted ones later. In his view, the four-year cryptocurrency cycle is dead. “I think the reason is because the rate of emission now is so small relative to the market capital of Bitcoin. And it’s, like, not really significant in the big scheme of things.”

data.title: Bitcoin Blasts Past $98,000: Is $100K Next, Or A Trap?
data.description:
Bitcoin has soared past the $98,000 on Thursday, fueling intense debate among traders over whether the $100K milestone is again within reach or if the current rally is vulnerable to a swift correction. Behind the scenes, market observers point to surging open interest (OI) and increased leverage, spotlighting the possibility of a leverage-driven push.

Bitcoin Rally Or Trap?

CryptoQuant community analyst Maartunn (@JA_Maartun) warned of a “leverage driven pump,” noting a $2.4B jump in Bitcoin’s OI within 24 hours. Via X, he wrote: “Leverage Driven Pump: $2.4B (7.2%) increase in Open Interest in Bitcoin over the past 24 hours.”

Confirming these observations, well-known crypto commentator Byzantine General (@ByzGeneral) highlighted the significant role of fresh long positions in propelling prices higher: “Lots of fresh longs coming in here on BTC which is shoving price higher. Kinda funny that the entire market is getting lifted right now off the back of these degen longs here.”

Analysts from alpha dojo (@alphadojo_net) echoed sentiments of caution, underscoring a notable gap between futures-based open interest and spot-driven purchases: “BTC continues to grind upwards, while the OI rises steadily, but there is little spot buying. BTC is now approaching the upper end of the range again. It seems that some market participants have tried to frontrun Saylor‘s planned $2 billion bid.”

Though the prospect of a large buy could propel the market, they warn that without fresh catalysts like a “short-term narrative or positive news, it currently looks like BTC will struggle to sustainably pump above the $100k mark.”

Renowned crypto analyst Bob Loukas provided a cyclical framework for interpreting Bitcoin’s price movements, noting that the market may be approaching the end of one multi-week cycle and the start of another: “We’re on verge of completing a Bitcoin Weekly Cycle, as I’ve been sharing last 6 weeks. For context, there have been just 5 weekly Cycles since the 2022 bear market lows. (Avg 6month events). 4 of these cycles had 90-105% moves. One failed to do much (June-Sept 23).”

When asked if this signals an imminent market top, Loukas clarified:“I’m saying we’re about to begin a new one. Cycles always begin from the lows.” His comments suggest that while a cycle transition is imminent, it does not necessarily equate to a market peak—rather, it could mark the start of a new uptrend.

Technical analyst Rekt Capital (@rektcapital) emphasized the significance of Bitcoin’s daily close above the $97,700 threshold, suggesting that a successful retest of this zone could pave the way for a move beyond $100,000: “The early-stage momentum generated by the Bullish Divergence has translated itself into this recent breakout move. And with the recent Daily Close above ~$97700, Bitcoin will now try to retest said level as support to enable trend continuation.”

He further elaborated on Bitcoin’s relative strength index (RSI) channel, implying that the break above a series of lower highs may signal the next leg up: “Over time, Bitcoin’s price continued to retest the blue trendline as support. And the RSI continued to hold its Channel Bottom. Lately, the RSI broke its series of Lower Highs, indicating that the RSI may be ready to uptrend to the Channel Top.”

Looking ahead, a clear retest of $97,700 as support could confirm Rekt Capital’s bullish outlook: “Daily Close above $97700 has been successful (light blue). Any dips into $97700 would constitute a retest attempt. A post-breakout retest of $97700 into new support would fully confirm the breakout to position BTC for a rally to $101k resistance.”

At press time, BTC traded at $98,645.

data.title: April WTI Crude Oil Contract Closes Down US$2.08; Settles at US$70.40 per Barrel
Bybit CEO says firm secured almost 80% of lost ETH as bridge loan from partners to ease liquidity crunch following $1.4 billion hack
data.description:
Bybit Co-Founder and CEO Ben Zhou responded to audience questions about how the firm will move forward with customer withdrawals following its $1.4 billion hack Friday morning. 

Zhao said in an X Space livestream that all client withdrawals will be processed, even if they are under review. The firm will not buy Ethereum but instead rely on a bridge loan, or a type of short-term loan to aid an entity's transition period, from its partners to facilitate the endeavor. 

"For immediate sake, we are currently reaching out to our partners to give us a bridge loan," Zhou said in a Friday livestream. "So, currently, we are not buying [Ethereum]. And even if we did want to buy, it is too big of amount to be moving around."

"But we are getting help, support, from our partners," Zhou continued. "We actually already secured almost 80% of the Ethereum that's been stolen as a bridge loan to give us that liquidity, to help us with the liquidity crunch, so we can pass this crucial period." 

Bybit confirmed it lost $1.4 billion in funds after a hacker gained access to the firm's multisig cold wallet, which one expert called "the largest crypto theft of all time," The Block had previously reported.

Bybit is a centralized exchange based in Singapore. The firm recorded $233.36 billion in spot market volume in January, The Block's Data Dashboard shows.
